page: Microsoft Azure DC4eds_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DCEDV6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor with 4 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs). The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. For local storage, the instance includes a 236 GB local disk and is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments.

This instance does not include integrated graphics processing units, making it unsuitable for GPU-accelerated workloads. It also does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and bundled 236 GB local storage provide a solid balance of resource density for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the overhead of shared-core configurations. The instance is designed for workloads such as small-scale databases, general-purpose web serving, enterprise application hosting, and development environments that benefit from dedicated resources and local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure DC4eds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCEDV6 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 4.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. The instance features 236 GB of bundled local storage and supports Premium Storage for demanding I/O requirements. It does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Positioned as a balanced resource option, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention. The combination of dedicated processing power and local storage capacity makes it an efficient choice for workloads that require predictable execution times. This server type is suitable for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, web serving, and application development environments requiring dedicated compute and local storage.
